Events & Deadlines
Upcoming Events
Ask A Student in MS in Applied Data Science
Are you curious about life as a student in the University of Chicago’s [MS in Applied Data Science](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/ms-in-applied-data-science) program? Do you have burning questions about [coursework](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/ms-in-applied-data-science/course-progressions), projects, or the overall student experience? Well, you’re in luck! We’re thrilled to announce our upcoming virtual info session, “Ask a Student,” where you can get all your queries answered by a current student. The MS in Applied Data Science “Ask a Student” info session allows prospective students the opportunity to meet virtually with current students in the MS in Applied Data Science program.
Featured Student: Eloi has been a full-time in-person student in the MS Applied Data Science program since Fall 2025. Originally from Canada, he enjoys practicing different sports such as racquetball and cross-country running. In his previous roles in private equity and financial holdings, Eloi optimized balance sheets for large asset-intensive firms using advanced analytics, developed strategies for structured products, and managed end-to-end M&A processes. Eloi plans to leverage his studies to decuple his impact and scale his expertise across diverse industries.
Date: April 20th, 2026
Time: 6:00 PM – 7:00 PM (CST)
Registration: [Click Here](https://apply-psd.uchicago.edu/register?id=45059d23-738b-4dc3-bb2d-8916ce628489)
💡 Attend this event to receive an application fee waiver for the Online MS in Applied Data Science program. After the session, you’ll receive a code via email that can be applied once your application is submitted.
Info Session – MS in Applied Data Science (In-Person Program)
Join us on April 21st for a virtual information session featuring our MS in Applied Data Science Associate Clinical Professor, [Arnab Bose](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/people/arnab-bose-phd), and Assistant Director of Enrollment Management, Matthew Harris-Ridker, to learn more about the in-person program. This session is designed to provide valuable insights into the program’s curriculum, admission requirements, scholarship opportunities, and more!
Date: April 21st, 2026
Time: 5:00 pm – 6:00 pm (CST)
Registration: [Click Here](https://apply-psd.uchicago.edu/register?id=0275a4fe-3708-439d-b316-c0a11edf6d32)
Data Science Seminar Series – An Introduction to Computer Vision Techniques and Applications
In this Data Science Seminar Series, you will hear from MS-ADS alumni Steve Veldman, who is a Lead Machine Learning Engineer at Rotational Labs. The Seminar Series presents projects, research, and questions posed by our instructors. Learn how UChicago does data science, while seeing how our instructors and students set us apart.
Speaker Bio: A veteran of the manufacturing industry, [Steve Veldman](https://www.linkedin.com/in/steve-veldman) is passionate about building practical and impactful solutions to real-world problems. In his role as a Lead Machine Learning Engineer at Rotational Labs, Steve helps to make the power of machine learning and artificial intelligence accessible to organizations of all sizes by building a meaningful understanding of his clients’ goals and challenges. He specializes in computer vision as well as classical machine learning techniques, and has a keen interest in the developing field of multimodal AI. Outside of work, Steve enjoys playing guitar, running, hiking, and cultivating an eclectic vinyl record collection.
Presentation Summary: This webinar breaks down the fundamental tasks of computer vision, explaining the mechanics behind how neural networks, transformers, and the latest vision-language models (VLMs) interpret visual data. We’ll explore real-world applications, demonstrating how specific CV techniques can be applied to use cases as complex and varied as interpreting medical images, monitoring security camera feeds, and digitizing paper documents. We will also discuss the exciting shift toward multimodal LLMs and how they are expanding what’s possible in visual reasoning. Whether you are looking to understand the core technology or its practical impact on the field, this session will provide a strong foundation for navigating the rapidly evolving landscape of computer vision.
Date: April 29th, 2026
Time: 5:00 PM – 6:00 PM (CST)
Registration: [Click Here](https://apply-psd.uchicago.edu/register?id=fa283f7b-aab2-4891-9968-7b90a89786e8)
💡 Attend this event to receive an application fee waiver for the Online MS in Applied Data Science program. After the session, you’ll receive a code via email that can be applied once your application is submitted.
Info Session – MS in Applied Data Science (Online Program)
Ready to explore an advanced degree in a rapidly growing field? Join us on May 13th for a virtual information session featuring Senior Assistant Director of Enrollment [Patrick Vonesh](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/people/patrick-vonesh) and Associate Clinical Professor [Nick Kadochnikov](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/people/nick-kadochnikov) to learn more about the online Master’s in Applied Data Science program. This session is designed to provide valuable insights into the program’s curriculum, admission requirements, scholarship opportunities, and more!
Date: May 13th, 2026
Time: 5:00 PM – 6:00 PM (CST)
Registration: [Click Here](https://apply-psd.uchicago.edu/register?id=3df36788-afb0-4d10-91a3-c6c064af5de2)
Stay Connected!
Follow us on Instagram ([@dsi_uchicago](https://www.instagram.com/dsi_uchicago)) and TikTok ([@msads_uchicago](https://www.tiktok.com/@msads_uchicago))
Check out our Data Science Seminar Series on YouTube: [Link](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL0IrIAIuK93EonLgPKZ7oIcpt0p_j58vm) to Past SeminarsWatch our most recent seminar from March 26th ﻿
Watch our most recent seminar from March 26th ﻿
Deadlines
All program offerings, including the part-time online program, begin in Autumn. Curious about the academic calendar? Please use the following link to access current and future calendar years: [Academic Calendar](https://www.uchicago.edu/en/education-and-research/academic-calendar)
Update your resume to reflect your most recent experiences and achievements.
Connect with your recommenders to ensure you secure strong letters of recommendation.
Schedule a conversation with [Patrick Vonesh](https://apply-psd.uchicago.edu/portal/onlinemsads) and/or [Jose Alvarado](https://apply-psd.uchicago.edu/portal/inpersonmsads) to learn more about the program and discuss your goals.
Attend one of our upcoming, virtual events.
In-Person:
January 26, 2026 – International Application Deadline (requiring visa sponsorship from UChicago)
March 4, 2026 – Second Priority Application Deadline
May 6, 2026 – Third Priority Application Deadline
June 23, 2026 – Final Application Deadline
International applicants who require a new visa and did not apply by the January 2026 deadline must plan to apply in the next application cycle for Autumn 2027 entry.
Applicants who are currently studying in the United States on an F-1 visa and will transfer their SEVIS record from another U.S. institution may still apply by the remaining application deadlines.
Online:
Autumn 2026June 23, 2026 – Final Application Deadline
June 23, 2026 – Final Application Deadline
Online application decisions are released on a rolling basis. The deadline represents the last date applications are accepted for the respective start date.
*The application portal may close early if the cohort is filled.

content.text: Ask A Student in MS in Applied Data Science

Are you curious about life as a student in the University of Chicago’s [MS in Applied Data Science](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/ms-in-applied-data-science) program? Do you have burning questions about [coursework](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/ms-in-applied-data-science/course-progressions), projects, or the overall student experience? Well, you’re in luck! We’re thrilled to announce our upcoming virtual info session, “Ask a Student,” where you can get all your queries answered by a current student. The MS in Applied Data Science “Ask a Student” info session allows prospective students the opportunity to meet virtually with current students in the MS in Applied Data Science program.

Featured Student: Eloi has been a full-time in-person student in the MS Applied Data Science program since Fall 2025. Originally from Canada, he enjoys practicing different sports such as racquetball and cross-country running. In his previous roles in private equity and financial holdings, Eloi optimized balance sheets for large asset-intensive firms using advanced analytics, developed strategies for structured products, and managed end-to-end M&A processes. Eloi plans to leverage his studies to decuple his impact and scale his expertise across diverse industries.

💡 Attend this event to receive an application fee waiver for the Online MS in Applied Data Science program. After the session, you’ll receive a code via email that can be applied once your application is submitted.
Info Session – MS in Applied Data Science (In-Person Program)

Join us on April 21st for a virtual information session featuring our MS in Applied Data Science Associate Clinical Professor, [Arnab Bose](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/people/arnab-bose-phd), and Assistant Director of Enrollment Management, Matthew Harris-Ridker, to learn more about the in-person program. This session is designed to provide valuable insights into the program’s curriculum, admission requirements, scholarship opportunities, and more!
Data Science Seminar Series – An Introduction to Computer Vision Techniques and Applications

In this Data Science Seminar Series, you will hear from MS-ADS alumni Steve Veldman, who is a Lead Machine Learning Engineer at Rotational Labs. The Seminar Series presents projects, research, and questions posed by our instructors. Learn how UChicago does data science, while seeing how our instructors and students set us apart.

Speaker Bio: A veteran of the manufacturing industry, [Steve Veldman](https://www.linkedin.com/in/steve-veldman) is passionate about building practical and impactful solutions to real-world problems. In his role as a Lead Machine Learning Engineer at Rotational Labs, Steve helps to make the power of machine learning and artificial intelligence accessible to organizations of all sizes by building a meaningful understanding of his clients’ goals and challenges. He specializes in computer vision as well as classical machine learning techniques, and has a keen interest in the developing field of multimodal AI. Outside of work, Steve enjoys playing guitar, running, hiking, and cultivating an eclectic vinyl record collection.

Presentation Summary: This webinar breaks down the fundamental tasks of computer vision, explaining the mechanics behind how neural networks, transformers, and the latest vision-language models (VLMs) interpret visual data. We’ll explore real-world applications, demonstrating how specific CV techniques can be applied to use cases as complex and varied as interpreting medical images, monitoring security camera feeds, and digitizing paper documents. We will also discuss the exciting shift toward multimodal LLMs and how they are expanding what’s possible in visual reasoning. Whether you are looking to understand the core technology or its practical impact on the field, this session will provide a strong foundation for navigating the rapidly evolving landscape of computer vision.

💡 Attend this event to receive an application fee waiver for the Online MS in Applied Data Science program. After the session, you’ll receive a code via email that can be applied once your application is submitted.
Info Session – MS in Applied Data Science (Online Program)

Ready to explore an advanced degree in a rapidly growing field? Join us on May 13th for a virtual information session featuring Senior Assistant Director of Enrollment [Patrick Vonesh](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/people/patrick-vonesh) and Associate Clinical Professor [Nick Kadochnikov](https://datascience.uchicago.edu/people/nick-kadochnikov) to learn more about the online Master’s in Applied Data Science program. This session is designed to provide valuable insights into the program’s curriculum, admission requirements, scholarship opportunities, and more!
Follow us on Instagram ([@dsi_uchicago](https://www.instagram.com/dsi_uchicago)) and TikTok ([@msads_uchicago](https://www.tiktok.com/@msads_uchicago))
Check out our Data Science Seminar Series on YouTube: [Link](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL0IrIAIuK93EonLgPKZ7oIcpt0p_j58vm) to Past SeminarsWatch our most recent seminar from March 26th ﻿
Watch our most recent seminar from March 26th ﻿

All program offerings, including the part-time online program, begin in Autumn. Curious about the academic calendar? Please use the following link to access current and future calendar years: [Academic Calendar](https://www.uchicago.edu/en/education-and-research/academic-calendar)
Update your resume to reflect your most recent experiences and achievements.
Connect with your recommenders to ensure you secure strong letters of recommendation.

Schedule a conversation with [Patrick Vonesh](https://apply-psd.uchicago.edu/portal/onlinemsads) and/or [Jose Alvarado](https://apply-psd.uchicago.edu/portal/inpersonmsads) to learn more about the program and discuss your goals.
Attend one of our upcoming, virtual events.
January 26, 2026 – International Application Deadline (requiring visa sponsorship from UChicago)
International applicants who require a new visa and did not apply by the January 2026 deadline must plan to apply in the next application cycle for Autumn 2027 entry.
Applicants who are currently studying in the United States on an F-1 visa and will transfer their SEVIS record from another U.S. institution may still apply by the remaining application deadlines.
Online application decisions are released on a rolling basis. The deadline represents the last date applications are accepted for the respective start date.
*The application portal may close early if the cohort is filled.

## Deadlines
